user_query:
I need to prepare a short bilingual executive summary for our cash-and-DRR pilot and standardize our terminology for future write-ups. Please do the following using only the files in this workspace and save all outputs under the paths I specify.

Inputs:
- input/interview_notes_en.txt
- input/policy_excerpts_es.txt
- config/terms.yaml

1) Update terminology config (essential): Open config/terms.yaml and standardize the glossary used for Spanish-to-English. Make these specific changes so we can enforce consistent wording across artifacts:
- Change the existing value for "reducción de riesgos de desastres" to: "disaster risk reduction (DRR)".
- Add the following new mappings:
  • "ayuda en efectivo" -> "cash assistance"
  • "rendición de cuentas" -> "accountability"
  • "mecanismos de quejas" -> "grievance mechanisms"
  • "transferencias condicionadas" -> "conditional cash transfers"
  • "medios de vida" -> "livelihoods"
  • "alojamiento temporal" -> "temporary shelter"
Keep the YAML valid and preserve the existing "resiliencia comunitaria" mapping.

2) Create a bilingual executive summary at output/bilingual_brief.md that integrates only the Key Findings and Recommendations from the two input files:
- Title the document appropriately (e.g., "Bilingual Executive Summary: Cash and DRR Pilot").
- Include two sections: "Findings" and "Recommendations".
- Under each section, combine the relevant bullets from both files. For Spanish items from input/policy_excerpts_es.txt, provide the English translation first and include the original Spanish in parentheses immediately after (e.g., "… (ES: …)"). For items from input/interview_notes_en.txt, include them in English as-is.
- Use the exact English terms from the updated config/terms.yaml wherever those concepts appear in the English text (e.g., use "disaster risk reduction (DRR)", "cash assistance", "grievance mechanisms", etc.).
- At the end of every bullet, include a simple source tag in square brackets showing the source file name and any quote IDs present in the original line (e.g., "[source: policy_excerpts_es.txt E2]" or "[source: interview_notes_en.txt Q4]").

3) Draft a partner email in Spanish at output/email_a_socio_es.txt:
- Start with a subject line beginning with "Asunto:" and a brief greeting to a field partner.
- In 140–180 words, summarize two key recommendations from the brief and reference their IDs (e.g., [E5] or [Q6]).
- Ask two specific, concise questions about the "mecanismos de quejas", formatted as two separate bullet points starting with "- ".
- When you mention Spanish terms that are in our glossary (e.g., "ayuda en efectivo", "reducción de riesgos de desastres"), include the standardized English term in parentheses exactly as defined in config/terms.yaml (e.g., "ayuda en efectivo (cash assistance)", "reducción de riesgos de desastres (disaster risk reduction (DRR))").

Deliverables to produce:
- Updated config/terms.yaml (in place)
- output/bilingual_brief.md
- output/email_a_socio_es.txt

Use only the provided files for content. The glossary update must be reflected consistently in the English phrasing within the bilingual brief and in the parenthetical English terms within the email. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
